state_detector_rootcause_report.symptom: StateDetector returns 100% HOT in sample idx 700-749

entropy.note: entropy < 2.05 triggers HOT vote in 369/600 periods (61.5%)

volatility.note: volatility < 0.55 triggers HOT vote in 53/600 periods (8.8%)

regime_shift.note: No regime shifts detected in full range

4_anomalies_detected.detail: MetaStrategyV2._calc_state_features hardcodes stability=0.5 when prediction_history < 10. This means stability never varies, never triggers TRANSITION from stability signal.
No period in 600-sample reaches entropy > 2.40, so CHAOTIC never gets entropy votes. CHAOTIC threshold may be too high for this dataset.
COLD requires volatility > 0.85 (mean of last 30). Only 51/600 periods (8.5%) meet this. Combined with no CHAOTIC votes, HOT dominates.

5_root_cause_determination.explanation: StateDetector is working as designed. The thresholds are not 'wrong' but they are misaligned with this dataset's characteristics. The entropy threshold for HOT (2.05) is triggered by 61.5% of periods, while CHAOTIC threshold (2.40) is never met. This causes systematic HOT bias.
5_root_cause_determination.data_vs_code_issue: DATA_CHARACTERISTIC - entropy in this dataset rarely exceeds 2.05, making HOT the dominant state

comparison_with_v1_1_stable.drift_explanation: The +0.062 drift is NOT caused by StateDetector - it's caused by zodiacs column data format adaptation (rolling window from raw_zodiac). StateDetector is functioning within its design parameters given the data.

state_detector_rootcause_report.note: Root cause is threshold design issue, not code bug. System is functioning correctly given data characteristics. Fix would require threshold adjustment but is prohibited under SYSTEM_FROZEN.
